title: Udharo (credit on account)
content: Udharo diyeko = credit SALE → Dr Debtor, Cr Sales. Udharo liyeko = credit PURCHASE → Dr Purchase, Cr Creditor. Ask: diyeko ho ki linubhayeko? Examples: Ram lai 3000 udharo diyeko; Shyam store bata 10000 udharo ma liyeko.
source: Nepal Accounting KB Phase 1 (AI Training — verify tax/legal with IRD/CA)

title: Saapati (informal loan)
content: Informal loan. Received: Dr Cash, Cr Loan Payable. Given: Dr Loan Receivable, Cr Cash. NOT income/expense. vs capital: ask firtaa dinu parchha?
source: Nepal Accounting KB Phase 1 (AI Training — verify tax/legal with IRD/CA)

title: Jamma gareko (deposit)
content: Usually bank deposit = contra Dr Bank, Cr Cash. NOT income. Clarify kaha jamma?
source: Nepal Accounting KB Phase 1 (AI Training — verify tax/legal with IRD/CA)

title: Nikaale (withdrew)
content: Bank withdrawal: Dr Cash, Cr Bank. Owner personal: Dr Drawings, Cr Cash. Expense: Dr Expense, Cr Cash. Clarify purpose.
source: Nepal Accounting KB Phase 1 (AI Training — verify tax/legal with IRD/CA)

title: Maal aayo (goods arrived)
content: Purchase — ask cash/credit, VAT bill, supplier name. Dr Purchase/Inventory, Cr Cash/Creditor.
source: Nepal Accounting KB Phase 1 (AI Training — verify tax/legal with IRD/CA)

title: Maal gayo (goods sold/out)
content: Usually SALE — cash or credit? Or return to supplier? Ask bicheko ho ki return?
source: Nepal Accounting KB Phase 1 (AI Training — verify tax/legal with IRD/CA)

title: Bill katein (invoice issued)
content: Issuing invoice = sales. VAT bill or simple? Who, amount, cash/credit?
source: Nepal Accounting KB Phase 1 (AI Training — verify tax/legal with IRD/CA)

title: Bill aayo (supplier invoice)
content: Purchase bill received — payment made or pending? VAT on bill?
source: Nepal Accounting KB Phase 1 (AI Training — verify tax/legal with IRD/CA)

title: Bhuktaani (payment made)
content: Payment to supplier, expense, or loan. Ask kaslai, kati, cash/bank.
source: Nepal Accounting KB Phase 1 (AI Training — verify tax/legal with IRD/CA)

title: Paisa baanki chha (outstanding)
content: Receivable (customer owes) or payable (owe supplier). Clarify direction.
source: Nepal Accounting KB Phase 1 (AI Training — verify tax/legal with IRD/CA)

title: Graahakle pachhi dincha
content: Credit sale — Dr Debtor, Cr Sales. Record customer name.
source: Nepal Accounting KB Phase 1 (AI Training — verify tax/legal with IRD/CA)

title: Sahule maageko
content: Supplier requesting payment — check creditor ledger.
source: Nepal Accounting KB Phase 1 (AI Training — verify tax/legal with IRD/CA)

title: Maal firta (return)
content: Customer return=sales return/credit note. Supplier return=purchase return. Reverse VAT if applicable.
source: Nepal Accounting KB Phase 1 (AI Training — verify tax/legal with IRD/CA)

title: Chhut (discount)
content: Trade discount often net in bill. Cash discount=discount allowed/received.
source: Nepal Accounting KB Phase 1 (AI Training — verify tax/legal with IRD/CA)

title: Noksan (loss)
content: Operating loss, inventory loss, bad debt — each different. Ask kun noksan?
source: Nepal Accounting KB Phase 1 (AI Training — verify tax/legal with IRD/CA)

title: Myaad gujriyo (expiry)
content: Expired inventory write-off: Dr Inventory Loss, Cr Inventory.
source: Nepal Accounting KB Phase 1 (AI Training — verify tax/legal with IRD/CA)

title: Tutphut bhayo (breakage)
content: Damage loss: Dr Loss on Damaged Goods, Cr Inventory.
source: Nepal Accounting KB Phase 1 (AI Training — verify tax/legal with IRD/CA)

title: Ghar kharcha liyeko
content: ALWAYS drawings: Dr Drawings, Cr Cash. NEVER expense. Most common Nepal SME error.
source: Nepal Accounting KB Phase 1 (AI Training — verify tax/legal with IRD/CA)

title: Byapar ko paisa aafno kaam ma
content: Owner drawings — Dr Drawings, Cr Cash/Bank. NOT expense.
source: Nepal Accounting KB Phase 1 (AI Training — verify tax/legal with IRD/CA)
